title: Replication, Multi-Region Access, and Disaster Recovery · Mastery Check
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for replication, multi-region access, and disaster recovery and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for multi-region recovery dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure versioning, destination ownership, IAM, KMS, filters, delete-marker behavior, replication status, metrics, and failure remediation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate Multi-Region Access Points, latency routing, active-active writes, consistency, replication lag, failover controls, and client endpoint support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Durability Availability Recovery · changed case · Quiz Durability Av…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 9165 during a regional recovery and budget exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate replication, isolated backup, restore, and failover state evidence; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
